description: Template renders a single variable.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('Hello {{ name }}!')
print(t.render(name='Alice'))

description: Template supports dot notation for attribute and key access.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
class Obj:
    x = 'attr_val'
t = Template('{{ obj.x }} and {{ d.key }}')
print(t.render(obj=Obj(), d={'key': 'dict_val'}))

description: Template substitutes multiple variables at once.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ a }} {{ b }} {{ c }}')
print(t.render(a='1', b='2', c='3'))

description: Template renders static text unchanged.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('no variables here')
print(t.render())

description: If block renders content when truthy; else branch when falsy.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if x %}yes{% else %}no{% endif %}')
print(t.render(x=1))
print(t.render(x=0))

description: If/elif/else chain selects first truthy branch.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if a %}A{% elif b %}B{% else %}C{% endif %}')
print(t.render(a=0, b=1))
print(t.render(a=0, b=0))

description: If supports comparison operators (== and <).
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t1 = Template('{% if a == b %}equal{% else %}diff{% endif %}')
print(t1.render(a=1, b=1))
print(t1.render(a=1, b=2))
t2 = Template('{% if x < y %}less{% endif %}')
print('[' + t2.render(x=1, y=2) + ']')
print('[' + t2.render(x=2, y=1) + ']')

description: For loop iterates over a list and renders each item.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% for i in items %}{{ i }},{% endfor %}')
print('[' + t.render(items=[1, 2, 3]) + ']')
print('[' + t.render(items=[]) + ']')

description: For loop with else branch renders else on empty sequence.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% for i in items %}{{ i }}{% else %}empty{% endfor %}')
print(t.render(items=[1, 2]))
print(t.render(items=[]))

description: Block definition renders its body content.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('before{% block main %}content{% endblock %}after')
print(t.render())

description: With block creates scoped variable binding.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% with x = 5 %}X={{ x }}{% endwith %}')
print(t.render())

description: Environment.from_string produces a Template with same render behavior.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template, Environment
t1 = Template('{{ x }}')
t2 = Environment().from_string('{{ x }}')
print(t1.render(x='A'), t2.render(x='A'))
print(t1.render(x='X'), t2.render(x='X'))

description: Undefined variable renders as empty string.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('[{{ missing }}]')
print(t.render())

description: Unclosed if block raises syntax error at parse time.
Unclosed for block raises syntax error at parse time.
Template re-renders with different contexts producing independent output.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ name }} is {{ age }}')
print(t.render(name='Alice', age='30'))
print(t.render(name='Bob', age='25'))
print(t.render(name='Carol', age='40'))
expected_output:
Alice is 30
Bob is 25
Carol is 40

description: For loop renders each element from a list.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% for x in xs %}[{{ x }}]{% endfor %}')
print(t.render(xs=['a', 'b']))

description: Undefined variable in if condition is treated as falsy.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if missing %}yes{% else %}no{% endif %}')
print(t.render())

description: Conditional gates a for loop whose items are rendered via variable substitution.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if show %}{% for i in items %}{{ i }}{% endfor %}{% endif %}')
print('[' + t.render(show=True, items=['a', 'b']) + ']')
print('[' + t.render(show=False, items=['a', 'b']) + ']')

description: Environment.from_string and Template produce identical complex renders.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template, Environment
text = '{% if x %}{{ x }}{% else %}none{% endif %}'
t1 = Template(text)
t2 = Environment().from_string(text)
print(t1.render(x='A'), t2.render(x='A'))
print(t1.render(x=''), t2.render(x=''))
print(t1.render(x='B') == t2.render(x='B'))

description: Block and with-block both render; accumulated state across re-renders stays consistent.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% block a %}{{ x }}{% endblock %}{% with y = 2 %}{{ y }}{% endwith %}')
print(t.render(x='1'))
print(t.render(x='A'))
print(t.render(x='9'))

description: Block with variables renders correctly on each call with different values.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% block title %}{{ name }}{% endblock %}')
print(t.render(name='Page1'))
print(t.render(name='Page2'))

description: Same context always produces same output regardless of prior renders with different values.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% block main %}{{ title }}{% endblock %}')
r1 = t.render(title='A')
t.render(title='B')
r3 = t.render(title='A')
print(r1)
print(r3)
print(r1 == r3)

description: With block variable does not leak to outer scope.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{{ x }}{% with x = 5 %}X={{ x }}{% endwith %}{{ x }}')
print(t.render(x='outer'))

description: Parse error does not prevent subsequent valid template creation and render.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template, Environment
try:
    t = Template('{% if x %}bad')
except Exception:
    print('parse-error')
t = Template('{{ ok }}')
print(t.render(ok='fine'))

description: After a syntax error, a correct template with conditionals still works.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
try:
    t = Template('{% for x in xs %}no end')
except Exception:
    print('err')
t = Template('{% if show %}yes{% endif %}')
print(t.render(show=1))

description: Render output depends only on context, not on order of prior calls.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% if x %}X{% else %}O{% endif %}')
print(t.render(x=1))
print(t.render(x=0))
print(t.render(x=1))
print(t.render(x=0))

description: For-else branch depends on whether sequence is empty; result changes with data.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% for i in items %}{{ i }}{% else %}E{% endfor %}')
print(t.render(items=['a']))
print(t.render(items=[]))
print(t.render(items=['x', 'y']))

description: If, for, block, with, and variables all compose in one complex template.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% block head %}{% if show %}{% for i in items %}{% with v = i %}{{ v }}{% endwith %}{% endfor %}{% endif %}{% endblock %}')
print(t.render(show=1, items=['a', 'b', 'c']))

description: With-block with undefined value renders empty; block renders defined vars; both compose.
test_code:
from minitemplate import Template
t = Template('{% with v = extra %}[{{ v }}]{% endwith %}{% block b %}{{ name }}{% endblock %}')
print(t.render(name='Alice'))
print('<' + t.render() + '>')
